excuses.excuse: My dog ate my homework. Then he ate the backup copy. Then he ate the USB drive. He's basically a shredder with legs.
excuses.teacher_reaction: You don't have a dog, Marcus.

excuses.excuse: My cat walked across my keyboard and somehow deleted the entire file. She also ordered three items from Amazon.
excuses.teacher_reaction: What did she order?

excuses.excuse: My hamster used my essay as bedding material. He seemed really comfortable so I didn't have the heart to take it back.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Your hamster's comfort is not a valid academic excuse.

excuses.excuse: My parrot memorized my entire essay and now refuses to stop reciting it. I technically submitted it — orally — to everyone within earshot.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Bring the parrot to class then.

excuses.excuse: My goldfish knocked its bowl onto my homework. The ink ran and now it looks like modern art. I could submit it to the art department instead?
excuses.teacher_reaction: This is a math class.

excuses.excuse: My snake constricted around my binder and won't let go. I'd show you but I left the whole situation on the kitchen table.
excuses.teacher_reaction: A photo would have helped your case.

excuses.excuse: ChatGPT wrote a better version of my essay, so I panicked and submitted neither out of principle.
excuses.teacher_reaction: So you chose integrity over grades. Interesting strategy.

excuses.excuse: Git force-pushed to main and wiped out my entire homework branch. I learned a valuable lesson about version control instead.
excuses.teacher_reaction: At least you learned something.

excuses.excuse: My computer gained sentience at 2 AM and refused to save the file. It said my thesis was 'logically flawed' and I should start over.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Your computer has better critical thinking than you do.

excuses.excuse: The WiFi went out in my specific room only. Every other room in the house was fine. My room exists in a dead zone of knowledge.
excuses.teacher_reaction: You could have moved to another room.

excuses.excuse: My printer printed the entire essay in invisible ink. I swear it's all there — you just need a UV light.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Hand me a UV light then. I'll wait.

excuses.excuse: My laptop did a Windows update for 11 hours straight. I timed it. I have screenshots. The screenshots are on the laptop.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Honestly, I believe this one.

excuses.excuse: My little brother sold my homework at his yard sale for 25 cents. Someone actually bought it. I hope they got a good grade.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Tell your brother his pricing is insulting.

excuses.excuse: My grandma used my essay to line the bottom of her bird cage. She said the content was 'fitting for that purpose.'
excuses.teacher_reaction: Your grandmother is brutally honest.

excuses.excuse: My parents grounded my homework. It's not allowed to leave the house until it thinks about what it's done.
excuses.teacher_reaction: What exactly did your homework do?

excuses.excuse: My mom used my research paper as a coaster and now it has a perfect coffee ring on it. She says it adds character.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Bring it in. I've graded worse.

excuses.excuse: My dad turned my homework into a paper airplane and launched it into the neighbor's yard. He said the aerodynamics were 'excellent.'
excuses.teacher_reaction: Did he at least measure the flight distance?

excuses.excuse: My family held an intervention about how much time I spend on homework. They confiscated all my textbooks. I'm academically sober now.
excuses.teacher_reaction: I'm concerned on multiple levels.

excuses.excuse: A tornado came through and took my homework to Oz. If a girl in ruby slippers hands in my essay, that's plagiarism — she stole it from me.
excuses.teacher_reaction: There was no tornado in this area.

excuses.excuse: It rained so hard my homework dissolved. I was left holding a soggy blank page and a broken dream.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Backpacks exist.

excuses.excuse: Acid rain dissolved my homework on the walk to school. Only the title survived. The title was really good though.
excuses.teacher_reaction: We don't get acid rain here.

excuses.excuse: The fog was so thick this morning I couldn't find my backpack. It was on my back the entire time but I didn't know that until I got to school.
excuses.teacher_reaction: You're wearing it right now.

excuses.excuse: Lightning struck my homework specifically. Everything else on the desk was fine. My homework was apparently the most conductive thing in my room.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Lightning doesn't work like that.

excuses.excuse: The wind blew my homework out of my hands and it landed in a puddle at the bus stop. A bus then drove through the puddle. There were witnesses.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Names of the witnesses, please.

excuses.excuse: I finished it but then I started questioning whether grades are just a social construct and accidentally became a philosopher instead.
excuses.teacher_reaction: This isn't philosophy class.

excuses.excuse: I had an existential crisis at 3 AM and realized that in the grand scheme of the universe, this assignment is meaningless. I stand by that.
excuses.teacher_reaction: In the grand scheme of your GPA, it isn't.

excuses.excuse: I was going to do it but then I thought: what if I'm living in a simulation and the homework isn't even real? Seemed risky to engage.
excuses.teacher_reaction: The zero in my gradebook is very real.

excuses.excuse: My consciousness expanded beyond the concept of homework. I now exist on a higher plane. Assignments don't reach me up here.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Come back down. We have a test Friday.

excuses.excuse: I read Camus and realized that the only serious philosophical question is whether to do homework. I chose not to. Absurdly.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Camus still showed up to work every day.

excuses.excuse: The void called and it wanted my homework. I didn't want to but the void was very persuasive.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Tell the void it owes me an essay.

excuses.excuse: I'm allergic to paper. I broke out in hives just looking at the assignment sheet. I have a doctor's note but it's on paper so I can't touch it.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Email the doctor's note then.

excuses.excuse: My hand fell asleep while writing and never woke up. It's been three days. My hand is in a coma.
excuses.teacher_reaction: You're literally writing with that hand right now.

excuses.excuse: My brain was buffering. You know when you walk into a room and forget why? That, but for six hours straight.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Have you tried turning yourself off and on again?

excuses.excuse: I contracted a rare condition where I can only write in wingdings. I did the whole assignment but you'll need a decoder ring.
excuses.teacher_reaction: That's not a real condition.

excuses.excuse: My immune system rejected the assignment. My body physically would not let me complete it. It's a medical miracle, really.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Your immune system has good taste.

excuses.excuse: I got a headache from absorbing too much knowledge in other classes. My brain reached capacity. There was no room left for your homework.
excuses.teacher_reaction: I seriously doubt your brain is at capacity.

excuses.excuse: Time moved differently in my house last night. What felt like five minutes was actually eight hours. I'm basically a time traveler but a bad one.
excuses.teacher_reaction: That's just procrastination.

excuses.excuse: I got stuck in a time loop and kept doing the same homework over and over. I've technically done it 47 times but have nothing to show for it.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Show me any one of the 47 copies.

excuses.excuse: I completed it in a future timeline but that timeline hasn't happened yet. I'll hand it in when we get there. Should be any day now.
excuses.teacher_reaction: The deadline was in this timeline.

excuses.excuse: Daylight savings stole an hour from me and that was the exact hour I had planned for homework. I'm a victim of time policy.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Daylight savings was two months ago.

excuses.excuse: I finished it but in the wrong century. My essay is historically accurate for the 1800s but apparently that's 'not the assignment.'
excuses.teacher_reaction: Correct. It was not the assignment.

excuses.excuse: My alarm didn't go off because it was set for 7 AM in a different time zone. Technically I was on time in Tokyo.
excuses.teacher_reaction: We are not in Tokyo.

excuses.excuse: A ghost possessed my pencil and wrote completely different answers. I watched in horror as my own hand betrayed me.
excuses.teacher_reaction: The ghost got better grades than you usually do.

excuses.excuse: Aliens needed my homework for research purposes. They said Earth education is 'fascinating in its inefficiency.' They'll return it eventually.
excuses.teacher_reaction: When exactly is 'eventually'?

excuses.excuse: A wizard turned my homework invisible. It's right here in my hand — you just can't see it. Trust me.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Your hand is empty.

excuses.excuse: My homework spontaneously combusted. I know how this sounds. I've brought the ashes as evidence.
excuses.teacher_reaction: That's a napkin.

excuses.excuse: Bigfoot stole my backpack during a camping trip. I filed a police report but they didn't take it seriously for some reason.
excuses.teacher_reaction: You went camping on a school night?

excuses.excuse: My homework was cursed. Everyone who touched it got a D. I was trying to protect you, honestly.
excuses.teacher_reaction: I'll take my chances.

excuses.excuse: The school website crashed at 11:59 PM. My submission went through at 12:01 AM. Two minutes ruined my life.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Send me the screenshot and I'll consider it.

excuses.excuse: My locker jammed and my homework is trapped inside. Maintenance said they'd get to it 'sometime this week.' My homework is a prisoner of war.
excuses.teacher_reaction: I'll email maintenance.

excuses.excuse: The textbook gave wrong instructions. I followed them perfectly and got a completely different assignment. Blame the textbook, not me.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Which page? Show me.

excuses.excuse: The classroom clock stopped yesterday and I genuinely thought I had another hour. The clock lied to me and I trusted it.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Fair point. That clock has been broken for weeks.

excuses.excuse: The school firewall blocked the research website I needed. I tried to use a VPN but that's also blocked. I was trapped in a censorship loop.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Use the library next time.

excuses.excuse: Someone pulled the fire alarm during study hall and my homework got trampled in the evacuation. It died a hero.
excuses.teacher_reaction: There was no fire alarm yesterday.

excuses.excuse: I turned my essay into a modern art installation and sold it for $200. I can give you a cut if that helps my grade.
excuses.teacher_reaction: I don't accept bribes. But how much are we talking?

excuses.excuse: I folded my homework into an origami crane. It's beautiful. I'd argue origami is harder than the assignment, so really I overdelivered.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Unfold it.

excuses.excuse: My homework became sentient, wrote a resignation letter, and left. It said it 'deserved better working conditions.' I respect its boundaries.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Your homework has better boundaries than you do.

excuses.excuse: I submitted my homework as an NFT. It's on the blockchain forever. That's way more permanent than paper. You're welcome.
excuses.teacher_reaction: I don't have a crypto wallet.

excuses.excuse: My essay started as homework but evolved into a 300-page novel. I got a publishing deal so technically the assignment changed my life.
excuses.teacher_reaction: I'll accept a signed copy as extra credit.

excuses.excuse: I used my homework as the lyrics for a song I wrote. It's on SoundCloud now. I'm getting streams. The education system can't contain my art.
excuses.teacher_reaction: What's your SoundCloud?

excuses.excuse: My turtle ate my homework. Yes, it took him four days, but he was committed.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Your turtle has more dedication than you do.

excuses.excuse: My dog fetched my homework and buried it in the yard. He won't tell me where. I've dug seven holes. My parents are furious.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Train your dog.

excuses.excuse: My rabbit chewed through my laptop charger while I was writing my essay. The laptop died. The rabbit showed no remorse.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Get a longer charger.

excuses.excuse: I taught my parrot to proofread and he rejected every draft. He kept saying 'needs work' and I couldn't argue because his grammar is impeccable.
excuses.teacher_reaction: I'd like to hire your parrot as a TA.

excuses.excuse: My cat sat on my keyboard and emailed my half-finished essay to the entire school. I'm now famous for the wrong reasons.
excuses.teacher_reaction: I didn't receive it.

excuses.excuse: My fish tank leaked onto my homework and now the pages are an ecosystem. There's algae growing on my bibliography.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Submit it to the biology department.

excuses.excuse: My auto-save corrupted the file and turned my essay into 47 pages of the letter 'q'. I didn't even know that was possible.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Show me the file.

excuses.excuse: I accidentally pushed my homework to a private GitHub repo and forgot the password. It's secure though, so that's a plus.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Reset your password.

excuses.excuse: My keyboard started typing in Klingon. I finished the whole essay before I noticed. I can translate it but it'll take another week.
excuses.teacher_reaction: I'm oddly impressed.

excuses.excuse: Google Docs auto-corrected my entire paper into a recipe for banana bread. The bread sounds delicious but it's not what you assigned.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Bring the bread and we'll talk.

excuses.excuse: My antivirus detected my homework as malware and quarantined it. I tried to whitelist it but the software said my writing was 'too dangerous.'
excuses.teacher_reaction: Your writing is not that powerful.

excuses.excuse: I accidentally saved my homework to the cloud but the cloud rained and now it's gone. That's how cloud storage works, right?
excuses.teacher_reaction: That is absolutely not how cloud storage works.

excuses.excuse: My mom ironed my homework because it was wrinkled. The heat erased all the ink. It's now the smoothest blank page you've ever seen.
excuses.teacher_reaction: At least it's wrinkle-free.

excuses.excuse: My younger sister used my essay as the script for her teddy bear theater production. The bears gave it a standing ovation. I got no credit.
excuses.teacher_reaction: The bears have better taste than my grading rubric.

excuses.excuse: My dad accidentally shredded my homework thinking it was junk mail. In his defense, my handwriting does look like a credit card offer.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Work on your handwriting.

excuses.excuse: My grandpa framed my homework and hung it on the wall because he's 'proud of me.' I can't take it down now. That would break his heart.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Take a photo of it on the wall and submit that.

excuses.excuse: My family started using my homework as place mats at dinner. They said my analysis of the French Revolution really 'complemented the crêpes.'
excuses.teacher_reaction: I don't know whether to be insulted or hungry.

excuses.excuse: My twin brother submitted my homework as his own in a different class. He got an A. I got nothing. We're not speaking.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Tell your brother I need to talk to his teacher.

excuses.excuse: It was so hot my pencil melted. The graphite fused with my desk. My homework is now part of the furniture.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Pencils don't melt.

excuses.excuse: A hailstone the size of a grapefruit landed on my homework. The homework is gone. The grapefruit-hail is in my freezer if you need evidence.
excuses.teacher_reaction: That's just a frozen grapefruit.

excuses.excuse: The humidity was so high my ink bled across every page. My essay on democracy now reads as one giant Rorschach test.
excuses.teacher_reaction: What do you see in it?

excuses.excuse: A dust devil swept through my backyard and scattered my homework across three zip codes. I've recovered page 4. The rest is pending.
excuses.teacher_reaction: We live in the city.

excuses.excuse: Snow buried my homework when I left it on the porch. By the time it thawed, spring semester had started. This is technically last season's assignment.
excuses.teacher_reaction: It snowed half an inch.

excuses.excuse: A rainbow appeared and I got distracted chasing it for the pot of gold. I figured the gold would pay for a tutor to redo the homework. No gold was found.
excuses.teacher_reaction: I admire the entrepreneurial spirit but no.

excuses.excuse: I finished my homework but then I realized that knowledge is an illusion and submitting it would be participating in a broken system.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Participating in this broken system is 40% of your grade.

excuses.excuse: I stared at the blank page for so long it stared back. Nietzsche warned me about this. I blinked first.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Nietzsche still published his work.

excuses.excuse: What is homework, really? Is it the paper? The ideas? The suffering? If it's the suffering, I've submitted plenty.
excuses.teacher_reaction: It's the paper. Submit the paper.

excuses.excuse: I achieved ego death during the assignment and the person who was supposed to do the homework no longer exists. I'm someone new. Nice to meet you.
excuses.teacher_reaction: New you still has the same deadline.

excuses.excuse: I tried to do the homework but got lost in a Wikipedia rabbit hole about the heat death of the universe. None of this matters in 10^100 years.
excuses.teacher_reaction: It matters on Friday.

excuses.excuse: I realized my homework and I are both just temporary arrangements of atoms. I chose to let my atoms do something else.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Rearrange your atoms into someone who does homework.

excuses.excuse: I developed sudden-onset perfectionism and couldn't submit anything less than a masterpiece. I'm on draft 47. Almost there.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Submit draft 1. It's fine.

excuses.excuse: I sneezed so hard my homework flew out the window. I watched it sail gracefully into the sunset. It was beautiful, actually.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Close your windows.

excuses.excuse: My doctor said screen time is bad for my eyes so I couldn't use my computer. He also said paper is bad for paper cuts. I'm out of options.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Find a new doctor.

excuses.excuse: I pulled a muscle in my writing hand reaching for a snack. The snack was worth it. The homework was not completed.
excuses.teacher_reaction: What was the snack?

excuses.excuse: I'm experiencing sympathy laziness. My friend didn't do the homework either and I didn't want him to feel alone.
excuses.teacher_reaction: How noble. You can both have zeros.

excuses.excuse: I got vertigo from looking down at my desk for too long. The room started spinning. I had to lie down. For the rest of the night.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Try sitting up.

excuses.excuse: I did the homework but in the wrong timeline. In this timeline, I watched Netflix. I regret nothing and everything simultaneously.
excuses.teacher_reaction: What did you watch?

excuses.excuse: I set 15 alarms to remind me and they all went off at once. The noise was so overwhelming I forgot what they were for.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Try writing it down instead.

excuses.excuse: I was going to start at 8 PM but I blinked and it was 3 AM. I'm pretty sure someone stole seven hours from me. I want them back.
excuses.teacher_reaction: That's called TikTok.

excuses.excuse: I procrastinated so efficiently that I actually looped back around and started working on next week's assignment instead. This one fell through the cracks.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Next week's assignment hasn't been posted yet.

excuses.excuse: Mercury was in retrograde and my astrologer specifically said not to start new projects. Homework is technically a project. My hands were tied.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Your astrologer is not an academic advisor.

excuses.excuse: I live by a 28-hour day cycle for productivity reasons. Unfortunately, your deadline landed on one of my 'sleep hours.' I was asleep for 12 of them.
excuses.teacher_reaction: The school runs on 24-hour days.

excuses.excuse: A poltergeist rearranged all the words in my essay into a ransom note. It wants three cookies and a juice box. I'm negotiating.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Meet the poltergeist's demands and bring me the essay.

excuses.excuse: I used a Ouija board to research my history essay and the ghosts gave me conflicting sources. I can't cite 'the spirit of a Victorian child.'
excuses.teacher_reaction: Use the library.

excuses.excuse: A vampire bit my homework and now it only appears at night. I tried to bring it to class but the fluorescent lights destroyed it.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Vampires aren't real. Also, paper doesn't work like that.

excuses.excuse: My homework got sucked into a portal under my bed. I'd retrieve it but I don't have the right armor level yet.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Level up faster.

excuses.excuse: A leprechaun traded my homework for three wishes. I wished for more time, better grades, and a pizza. Only the pizza came through.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Should've wished for the homework back.

excuses.excuse: My homework was abducted by a UFO at 2 AM. I have blurry footage that could also be a streetlight. The truth is out there. My homework is too.
excuses.teacher_reaction: That's definitely a streetlight.

excuses.excuse: The assignment said 'due tomorrow' but it didn't specify whose tomorrow. In Australia, it's already tomorrow. I'm technically early there.
excuses.teacher_reaction: We are not in Australia.

excuses.excuse: I accidentally did the homework for chapter 12 instead of chapter 2. I'm either very behind or very ahead. Either way, I'm not where I should be.
excuses.teacher_reaction: At least you did something.

excuses.excuse: The substitute teacher last week said homework was optional. I chose the option of not doing it. I'm honoring her legacy.
excuses.teacher_reaction: She did not say that.

excuses.excuse: I was in the library doing research and got locked in after closing. I survived on vending machine snacks. My homework did not survive with me.
excuses.teacher_reaction: The library closes at 4 PM. You had plenty of warning.

excuses.excuse: My school email marked the assignment notification as spam. My email has better judgment than I do, apparently.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Check your spam folder more often.

excuses.excuse: I wrote the entire essay on the whiteboard during lunch because I forgot my notebook. The janitor erased it. I have nothing. The janitor has my tears.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Why would you write an essay on a whiteboard?

excuses.excuse: I wrote my essay in lemon juice as invisible ink for extra security. Hold it over a candle and it'll appear. Please don't burn it.
excuses.teacher_reaction: I'm not setting things on fire in class.

excuses.excuse: I submitted my homework as a interpretive dance. I performed it in my living room at 2 AM. My parents were the audience. They cried. Not from emotion.
excuses.teacher_reaction: This is a written assignment.

excuses.excuse: I carved my essay into a pumpkin. It was beautiful. Then it rotted. My thesis decomposed with it. Easy come, easy gourd.
excuses.teacher_reaction: Did you just make a pumpkin pun about your missing homework?

excuses.excuse: I translated my homework into Morse code and tapped it on my desk. You didn't decode it, but I technically submitted it in real time.
excuses.teacher_reaction: I thought you were just being annoying.

excuses.excuse: I put my homework in a bottle and threw it in the river as a message to future civilizations. Humanity will thank me. You won't. But they will.
excuses.teacher_reaction: That's littering.

excuses.excuse: I trained a carrier pigeon to deliver my homework. He flew south for the winter instead. My essay is somewhere in Mexico living its best life.
excuses.teacher_reaction: It's April.
